methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $40M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $400M - $2.0B.

data.narrative:
Astrolab is a Series A-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $40M+, founded by Jaret B. Matthews. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 5 public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including ClearSpace, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, and Sierra Space.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis uses the $40M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical for Series A progression, yielding $80M. The peer-median method examines four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: Cowboy Space Corporation ($2.0B Series B), Aalyria ($1.3B Series B), ClearSpace ($400M Series A), and Hubble Network ($400M Series B). This cohort yields a median valuation of $850M with a range of $400M to $2.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples is not applicable; the Space & Aerospace sector median trades at 42.9x revenue across the five public comps, but Astrolab's revenue is not disclosed.

The triangulated analysis across two methods supports a valuation range of $68M to $977M, with a median of $465M. Key uncertainties include whether peer comparables—particularly Series B-stage firms—appropriately adjust for Astrolab's earlier funding stage and capital efficiency profile. Revenue generation timing and near-term contract visibility remain critical value drivers not captured in this analysis.
